questions.prompt: Which of these is the strongest single signal of what your page is about?

choices.text: The number of times your target keyword appears in the body.
The <title> element — it influences both your SERP title link and how AI features label your page.

questions.explanation: The <title> is the highest-leverage piece of HTML on your page. It's the primary candidate for the clickable SERP title, a strong topical signal to Google's algorithm, and one of the first things AI summarizers read. Keyword density is largely ignored. The meta keywords tag has been ignored by Google for ~15 years. URL slug matters only weakly.

questions.prompt: Google is rewriting your title in the SERP to something other than what you put in the <title> tag. What's the most likely cause and fix?

choices.text: Google is punishing you — file a reconsideration request.

Your <title> probably misrepresents the page (clickbait, keyword stuffing, or wildly off-topic). The fix is to write an accurate, useful title — Google rewrites when your title doesn't reflect the content well.
It's random — try clearing your sitemap.
The page is over 300KB and Google can't read it.

questions.explanation: Google rewrites titles mainly when they're not useful or accurate — keyword stuffing, all-caps, clickbait, or significant mismatch with content. The fix is to write a better title that matches the page. Fighting the rewrite is wasted effort.
